executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 101 organizations publicly listed as victims in the past 48 hours, indicating a coordinated mass exploitation event. The Qilin ransomware gang leads with 21 victims, followed by Play (10), Clop (14), and SafePay (11). High-impact targets include Canada Goose (hit by both CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Eastern Townships School Board, Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, and National Money Mart (database leaked). Multiple threat actor groups are simultaneously targeting Canadian infrastructure across healthcare, education, manufacturing, financial services, and government sectors.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 21 Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), BioPharma Services, Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Questica, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, and multiple SMEs
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a severe ransomware crisis with 101 organizations publicly claimed as victims in the past 48 hours, representing a potential coordinated campaign or mass exploitation event. Critical infrastructure is heavily affected, including healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services, Thunder Bay Counselling), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart, Collège Supérieur De Montréal), and municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health, Workers Health & Safety Centre). No CCCS advisories were included in this dataset.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was targeted by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters). Active Green + Ross (consumer automotive services) and Kids & Company (childcare services) were also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers (insurance/financial services) was hit by Sinobi. Multiple accounting and financial service firms were targeted including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, and SMT Lawyers.

report_2w.executive_summary: Fortinet FortiClientEMS is under active exploitation via CVE-2026-35616, a critical vulnerability that allows remote code execution—Canada's CCCS has issued an official alert. A China-linked group (Storm-1175) is weaponizing zero-day vulnerabilities to deploy Medusa ransomware within hours of compromise. Flowise AI Agent Builder faces active attacks exploiting a CVSS 10.0 RCE flaw with over 12,000 instances exposed globally. Additionally, a disgruntled researcher publicly released 'BlueHammer,' an unpatched Windows zero-day enabling privilege escalation. Wynn Resorts disclosed that 21,000 employees were affected by a ShinyHunters breach.

items.description: Improper access control in Fortinet FortiClientEMS enabling remote code execution
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway
items.software_affected: Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: Zero-day vulnerability exploited by Storm-1175 in Medusa ransomware attacks
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Zero-day vulnerability exploited by Storm-1175 in Medusa ransomware attacks
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Unpatched Windows zero-day privilege escalation exploiting Windows Defender
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: Medium, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.name: Storm-1175 (China-linked)
items.targets: Organizations with web-facing assets globally, deploying Medusa ransomware
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), rapid exploitation of zero-day vulnerabilities within hours of initial compromise

items.name: REvil (GandCrab)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Wynn Resorts (21,000 employees affected), Hims & Hers customer data
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage Object), T1078 (Valid Accounts), support platform compromise

items.name: Silent Ransom Group
items.targets: Jones Day law firm (10 clients affected)
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), limited data exfiltration

items.name: Iran-linked actors
items.ttps: T1110.003 (Password Spraying), targeting Middle East government and commercial entities

items.name: TA416 (China-aligned)
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), espionage campaigns resumed mid-2025

items.name: Lazarus Group
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1204 (User Execution), fake job interviews (Contagious Interview)

items.name: Qilin and Warlock Ransomware
items.ttps: T1562.001 (Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools), using vulnerable drivers to disable 300+ EDR solutions

items.name: North Korea IT workers
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), recruitment fraud involving Iranian workers for IT job fraud

canada_landscape.summary: Canada's Cyber Centre (CCCS) issued two critical alerts: AL26-007 for actively exploited Fortinet FortiClientEMS (CVE-2026-35616) and AL26-006 for Citrix NetScaler vulnerabilities. No direct incidents involving major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported during this period. Canadian military members were noted to have violated intelligence-gathering rules during COVID-19, though this pertains to operational security rather than cyber incidents.
